etymology.description: semantic 木(木) (mù, "tree") + phonetic 昜(昜) (yáng) -> a kind of tree
etymology.interpretation: The character 楊(楊) (yáng, "surname Yang") contains the semantic component 木(木) (mù, "tree"), indicating its relation to trees or wood, and the phonetic component 昜(昜) (yáng), contributing the pronunciation. It originally represented a type of tree, later used as a surname.
etymology.simplification: 楊(楊) was simplified to 杨(楊) by replacing the complex tree radical 木(木) (mù, "tree") in 昜(昜) (yáng) with the simplified 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water") radical for ease of writing.

examples.chinese: 杨先生是我的老师(楊先生是我的老師)。
examples.pinyin: Yáng xiānshēng shì wǒ de lǎoshī.

examples.chinese: 杨树在春天开满了白花(楊樹在春天開滿了白花)。
examples.pinyin: Yáng shù zài chūntiān kāi mǎn le báihuā.
examples.english: Poplar trees bloom with white flowers in spring.

examples.chinese: 他的朋友姓杨(他的朋友姓楊)，住在上海(住在上海)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de péngyǒu xìng Yáng，zhù zài Shànghǎi.
examples.english: His friend's surname is Yang and he lives in Shanghai.
